description: Functional words used to link ideas, indicate time, or modify sentences.

items.meaning: In spite of the fact that; even though.
items.example: Although it was raining, we went for a walk.

items.meaning: Used to introduce a statement that contrasts with the previous one.
items.example: I wanted to go; however, I had too much work.
He studied hard; therefore, he passed the exam.

items.meaning: During the time that; at the same time as.
items.example: Please be quiet while I am working.

items.meaning: Throughout the course of a period of time.
items.example: We met many people during our holiday.
We will be late unless we leave now.

items.meaning: From a particular time in the past until now; because.
items.example: I have lived here since 2020.

items.meaning: Before now or before a specific time.
items.example: I have already finished my homework.

items.meaning: Up to this time (usually in negative or questions).
items.example: Have you eaten yet?
It is still raining.
Have you ever been to Paris?
I have never seen that movie.
Sometimes we go to the cinema.
I usually drink coffee in the morning.
I have recently moved to a new house.
Suddenly, the lights went out.
It will probably snow tomorrow.
I like music, especially jazz.

items.meaning: After a long time; at last.
items.example: We finally arrived at the hotel.
Please call me immediately.
